title: How Sound Made
explanation: Vibrations make sound. Bigger vibrations make louder sound. Vibrations travel through air water or solid to our ears.
funFact: Put rice on a drum and hit it — watch rice jump from vibrations!

title: High Low Pitch
prompt: Short ruler over desk edge plucked — high or low pitch?
explanation: Shorter, tighter, thinner vibrate faster giving higher pitch. Longer slower gives lower pitch.
funFact: A guitar uses thick strings for low notes and thin strings for high notes!

title: Loudness Test
explanation: Gentler hit makes smaller vibrations so quieter volume. Further away also quieter.
funFact: Sound cannot travel in space because there is no air — total silence!

title: String Telephone
prompt: String telephone works best when string is...
explanation: Vibrations travel better through tight solid string than loose floppy string or air alone.
funFact: Whales can hear each other hundreds of kilometres away underwater because sound travels well in water!

title: Quiet Room
prompt: Which material best muffles sound for insulation?
explanation: Soft thick materials absorb vibrations and muffle sound. Hard thin materials let sound through or reflect.
funFact: Recording studios use egg-box shaped foam to trap sound echoes!

title: Echo Test
prompt: Where would echo be loudest clearest?

options: Large empty hall with hard walls
Small carpeted bedroom full of soft furniture
Outside open field with no walls

explanation: Hard smooth surfaces reflect sound well making echoes. Soft absorbs sound reducing echo.
funFact: Clap in empty swimming pool for huge echo!

title: Far Away Sound
prompt: How to make sound quieter to listener far away?

options: Move further away or make smaller vibrations

explanation: Sound gets quieter with distance and with smaller vibrations. Louder needs bigger vibrations.
funFact: Whisper only travels few metres, shout travels hundreds!

title: Long Ruler Low Note
prompt: Long ruler over desk plucked makes what pitch compared to short ruler?
explanation: Longer looser thicker vibrates slower giving lower pitch. Shorter tighter thinner faster higher pitch.
funFact: Double bass has very long thick strings for very low notes!

title: Sound Proofing
prompt: Which material best to soundproof recording studio reduce echo?

options: Thick soft foam egg box shape

explanation: Soft thick porous materials absorb sound vibrations reducing echo and muffling.
funFact: Egg box foam shape traps sound waves bouncing many times losing energy!

title: Space Silence
prompt: Why can astronauts not hear each other shouting in space outside spaceship?

options: No air particles in vacuum so sound cannot travel

explanation: Sound needs medium particles to vibrate through air water solid. Space vacuum has almost no particles so no sound travel.
funFact: In movies space explosions make noise but real space is totally silent!
